title: Beyond GANs and VAEs
concept: Diffusion models represent a fundamentally different generative approach: instead of learning to generate directly, they learn to gradually reverse a noising process.

exercises.prompt: If you slowly blur a photo until it's pure static, could you teach a machine to reverse that process step-by-step and get the photo back?

options.text: Yes, if it learns what noise was added at each step
No, once it's static it's gone forever
Only if the photo was black and white

exercises.explain: That's exactly the big idea behind diffusion models — learn to undo noise, one small step at a time!

cards.title: What if you learned by destroying things?
cards.body: Imagine ruining a picture on purpose, bit by bit, until it's total noise. Now imagine studying that process so closely that you can run it backwards — turning pure noise back into a picture. That backwards trick is how diffusion models create art, photos, and more.

cards.title: Diffusion Model
cards.body: A diffusion model is an AI that learns to create new things by reversing a noising process. First, it studies how real images slowly turn into random noise when noise is added step by step. Then it learns to do the opposite: starting from pure noise and removing a tiny bit of noise at a time until a clear image appears.

cards.title: Melting Ice, Then Freezing It Back
cards.body: Think of a detailed ice sculpture melting into a puddle — that's like adding noise, step by step, until nothing recognizable is left. A diffusion model is like an artist who studied every stage of that melt so carefully that they can now freeze a puddle back into the exact sculpture, one careful layer at a time.

cards.title: From Static to a Sunset
cards.body: You type 'a sunset over mountains.' The diffusion model starts with a canvas of random fuzzy noise. Step by step — often 20 to 50 steps — it removes a little noise each time, guided by your text prompt, until the fuzzy static slowly sharpens into a clear, colorful sunset picture.

cards.title: Myth: Diffusion models draw the image directly, like a GAN does in one shot.
cards.body: Truth: Diffusion models don't paint the picture in a single pass. They take many small denoising steps, gradually refining a noisy image into a clean one — more like slowly developing a photograph than painting it in one stroke.

cards.title: Two Different Roads to a New Image
cards.body: GANs and diffusion models both create new images, but they take very different paths to get there.

left.text: A generator and a critic compete in one quick face-off to produce an image in a single pass.

right.text: Noise is removed gradually over many small steps, slowly revealing a clear image.

exercises.prompt: What is the core idea behind how a diffusion model generates images?

options.text: It copies pixels from training images directly
It starts from noise and gradually removes noise in small steps
It uses two networks competing against each other
It generates the whole image in a single instant pass

exercises.explain: Diffusion models reverse a noising process step by step, slowly turning random noise into a clear image.

exercises.prompt: Diffusion models generate an image in one single step, just like flipping a switch.
exercises.explain: False! Diffusion models use many small steps, gradually removing noise until a clear image emerges.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the idea about diffusion models.
exercises.sentence: Diffusion models learn to gradually reverse a ______ process to create new images.
exercises.explain: Diffusion models learn to reverse a noising process — undoing noise little by little to reveal an image.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best captures why diffusion models are considered fundamentally different from GANs?

options.text: They learn to gradually undo noise instead of generating directly in one shot
They only work with black and white images
They don't require any training data at all

exercises.explain: The key difference is the approach: diffusion models denoise step-by-step rather than generating an image directly like a GAN's generator does.

exercises.prompt: A friend says, 'Diffusion models must work just like GANs, since both make new images.' What's the best way to explain why that's not quite right?

options.text: GANs use a generator vs. critic competition to make images in one pass, while diffusion models slowly remove noise over many steps
GANs and diffusion models are actually the exact same algorithm with different names
Diffusion models don't need any training, but GANs do
GANs can only make text, while diffusion models only make images

exercises.explain: GANs pit a generator against a critic to produce images directly, while diffusion models take a completely different route — learning to reverse a step-by-step noising process to reveal an image gradually.
